title: Pharmacokinetics Compartment Model
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Pharmacokinetics Compartment Model: PK model fitting: estimate clearance CL, volume V, and absorption k_a from plasma concentration-time data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.pk_compartments…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; inter_individual_variability, protein_binding_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: PK parameters (CL, V_1, k_12, k_21, k_a, F) in 2-compartment model
E.observation_y: plasma concentration C(t_i) at N_samples sampling time points

W.regime: Existence of the recovered PK_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); inter_individual_variability dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Lognormal sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: AUC within 15% for well-designed PK study. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |inter_individual_variability|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_subjects * N_samples * N_params) per NLME fit per iteration
